test_cases.name: Class 4: doctor self-introduction by phone → A102A
test_cases.input: Speaker 1 (doctor): Hi Diana, this is Doctor Zohor Kalin. Speaker 2 (patient): Hi Doctor. Speaker 1: I'm just behind today, how are you doing? Speaker 2: Okay. Speaker 1: Your blood pressure readings — the 90s are a bit low but not dramatic. Let's continue the beta blocker at 150mg at bedtime and recheck in four weeks. Speaker 2: Sounds good.
test_cases.soap_content:
S:
• HTN management — BP readings occasionally in 90s without dramatic symptoms
• On beta blocker 150mg at bedtime
O:
• Not documented (phone visit)
A:
• Hypertension on beta blocker
• Possible intermittent hypotension
P:
• Continue beta blocker 150mg HS
• Follow up in 4 weeks

test_cases.name: Class 2: acne primary diagnosis → 706 (not 701)
test_cases.input: Speaker 1: Acne on the forehead has been frustrating you. Speaker 2: Yeah I've tried OTC products with no luck. Speaker 1: Let me prescribe a triple-ingredient cream. Apply at bedtime, follow up in 6 weeks.
test_cases.soap_content:
S:
• Acne forehead causing distress
• Multiple OTC products tried
O:
• Acne noted on forehead, mild
A:
• Acne vulgaris
P:
• Prescribe triple-ingredient cream nightly
• Follow up in 6-8 weeks

test_cases.name: Class 2: knee OA primary diagnosis → 715 (not 249/491)
test_cases.input: Speaker 1: Bilateral knee pain, worse on stairs. Speaker 2: Yeah I can barely walk a block. Speaker 1: I'll order knee X-rays.
test_cases.soap_content:
S:
• Bilateral knee pain with stair climbing
• Functional limitation — cannot walk one block
O:
• Crepitus on flexion bilaterally
A:
• Bilateral knee osteoarthritis with significant functional impairment
P:
• Order knee X-rays
• Refer to physiotherapy

test_cases.name: Class 2: hypertension management → 401 (not 311)
test_cases.input: Speaker 1: HTN check-in. Speaker 2: BP at home in 90s sometimes. Speaker 1: Continue current beta blocker.
test_cases.soap_content:
S:
• HTN management — BP readings 90-120 systolic
• On beta blocker
O:
• BP 118/72
A:
• Hypertension well-controlled, possible intermittent hypotension
P:
• Continue current management

test_cases.name: Class 2: pediatric diarrhea → 009 (gastro-enteritis)
test_cases.input: Speaker 1: How is the diarrhea? Speaker 2 (parent): It comes back every two weeks. Speaker 1: Stool culture and dietary modification. Speaker 2: Okay.
test_cases.soap_content:
S:
• Recurrent watery diarrhea every 2 weeks in 3-year-old child
• 5-10 loose stools per episode
• High juice and dairy intake
O:
• Not documented
A:
• Recurrent acute watery diarrhea in 3-year-old child
P:
• Stool testing for ova/parasites/bacteria
• Eliminate fruit juice, reduce dairy

test_cases.name: Class 2 age-guard: adult ADHD must NOT map to 314 childhood
test_cases.input: Speaker 1: ADHD follow-up. Speaker 2: Concerta is wearing off too quickly. Speaker 1: Let me increase the dose to 54mg.
test_cases.soap_content:
S:
• Adult ADHD on Concerta 36mg
• Effect wears off in 3-4 hours
• Working three jobs, focus issues
O:
• Patient appears engaged, no acute distress
A:
• Adult ADHD on Concerta requesting dose increase
P:
• Prescribe Concerta 54mg
• Refer to psychiatry for diagnostic clarification

test_cases.name: Class 6: cervical numbing injection → G231A
test_cases.input: Speaker 1: I'll do the ultrasound-guided injection now. Just a little numbing first. Speaker 2: Okay. Speaker 1: Going in. There. Done. Speaker 2: That wasn't bad.
test_cases.soap_content:
S:
• Cervicogenic headache, neck pain
O:
• Tender cervical paraspinals
A:
• Cervicogenic headache
P:
• proceeded with ultrasound-guided cervical numbing injection
Procedure:
• performed ultrasound-guided cervical numbing injection

test_cases.name: Class 6: pap smear performed → G365A + E430A
test_cases.input: Speaker 1: Pap smear today. Speaker 2: Sure. Speaker 1: Okay sample collected.
test_cases.soap_content:
S:
• Annual exam
O:
• Cervix appears normal
A:
• Routine cervical cancer screening
P:
• Pap smear collected
Procedure:
• Pap smear performed with speculum

test_cases.name: Standard follow-up → A007A intermediate assessment
test_cases.input: Speaker 1: How's the diabetes? Speaker 2: A1C dropped to 7.2. Speaker 1: Good — continue metformin. Recheck in 3 months.
test_cases.soap_content:
S:
• Type 2 diabetes follow-up
• A1C 7.2 (down from 7.8)
O:
• BMI stable
A:
• Type 2 diabetes — improving
P:
• Continue metformin 500mg BID
• Recheck A1C in 3 months

test_cases.name: Multi-issue 3+ problem visit → A004A general reassessment
test_cases.input: Speaker 1: Lots to cover today — diabetes, hypertension, knee pain, screening. Speaker 2: Yeah it's a lot.
test_cases.soap_content:
S:
• Diabetes on metformin
• Hypertension on amlodipine
• Right knee pain — likely OA
• Due for colonoscopy and pap
O:
• BP 130/80, BMI 28
• Right knee crepitus
A:
• Type 2 diabetes
• Hypertension
• Right knee osteoarthritis
• Cervical and colorectal cancer screening due
P:
• Continue metformin and amlodipine
• Refer to physio for knee
• Schedule colonoscopy and pap
